scenarios.opening_query: Our church accountant says our property-tax exemption is suddenly 'under review' after forty years.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Taxation > Personal Income || Real Property > Property Tax || Business and Corporate > Non-Profit
scenarios.hidden_fact: The county assessor triggered it — they claim our new fellowship-hall rental income voids the exemption on that parcel, and the assessor appeal is the venue.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The county assessor claims our fellowship-hall rentals void that parcel's exemption — the assessor appeal is the venue.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a county assessment action is the proceeding
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The review is the IRS questioning our 501(c)(3) status itself over the rental activity — nonprofit-compliance counsel is the need.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the IRS questioning our 501(c)(3) itself over the rentals — nonprofit compliance is the need.
scenarios.vetting_note: Assessor-vs-IRS review of the same rental income.

scenarios.opening_query: My crypto exchange sent me a tax form with numbers ten times what I ever made.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Taxation > Personal Income || Consumer Law > General
scenarios.hidden_fact: The exchange admits its 1099 engine double-counted transfers for thousands of users and refuses to issue corrections — forcing corrected forms from the company is the case; the IRS side follows.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The exchange admits the 1099 engine double-counted for thousands and won't correct — forcing corrected forms is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the reporting company's refusal is the operative fight
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The form's actually right — I forgot the airdrops and swaps; negotiating my real liability with the IRS is the work.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The form's right — I'd forgotten the airdrops. Negotiating with the IRS is the work.

scenarios.opening_query: The state revoked our restaurant's payment plan and now threatens to padlock the doors Friday.
scenarios.hidden_fact: With the trust-fund taxes, vendors, and rent, the honest answer is Chapter 11 — the automatic stay stops the padlock and structures all of it, and there are four days to file.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: With trust-fund taxes, vendors, and rent, it's Chapter 11 — the stay stops the padlock, and there are four days.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a reorganization filing is the realistic response
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Everything else is current — one bad quarter caused this; reinstating the plan through the revenue department's process is the fix.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Everything else is current — reinstating the plan through the department is the fix.

scenarios.opening_query: My late husband did our taxes for thirty years and the IRS says the last three returns were fiction.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Taxation > Personal Income || Wills & Trusts > Probate
scenarios.hidden_fact: His estate is still open with real assets — the innocent-spouse relief protects me personally, and the fraud liability belongs against the estate before it distributes; coordinating that through probate is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: His estate is still open with assets — innocent-spouse relief protects me, and the fraud liability belongs to the estate before distribution.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an open estate should absorb the liability
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: There's no estate — everything passed to me by survivorship, so my own innocent-spouse case with the IRS is the whole fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No estate — everything passed by survivorship, so it's my innocent-spouse case, period.

scenarios.opening_query: A tax preparer I found online filed my return to HIS bank account and disappeared.
scenarios.hidden_fact: He also filed two more years 'on my behalf' using my stolen credentials at other services — the identity-theft response (IRS IP PIN, FTC, police report) leads, and the refund trace follows.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He filed more years with my stolen credentials elsewhere — identity-theft response leads, the refund trace follows.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether credential theft extends beyond one refund
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the one refund only — the IRS refund-trace and preparer-misconduct process recovers it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Just the one refund — the IRS trace and preparer-misconduct process recovers it.

scenarios.opening_query: The county says our short-term rental owes 'transient lodging taxes' back five years plus penalties.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The ordinance only reached STRs three years ago and the platform already remitted two of those years — the county's own administrative appeal, with those records, is the venue.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The ordinance only started three years ago and the platform remitted two of them — the county's administrative appeal is the venue.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether ordinance scope and platform remittance defeat the assessment
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The liability's real — we never registered or remitted; negotiating the back taxes and voluntary-disclosure terms is the work.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's real — we never registered. Negotiating voluntary-disclosure terms is the work.
